system_description.overview: Openwealth, Inc develops an AI-powered compliance co-pilot that automates regulatory mapping, enabling organizations to move from regulation to product in minutes instead of months. The scope includes design, engineering, and maintenance of proprietary AI technology, data processing infrastructure, and automation tools that securely analyze regulations, map requirements, and generate actionable compliance outputs for product teams.

products.AI Compliance Co-Pilot: Automates regulatory mapping; analyzes regulations, maps requirements, and generates actionable compliance outputs for product teams

red_flags: Document is NOT an ISO 27001 audit report or certificate — it is only a Certification Agreement Form (pre-audit contract) with Gradient Certification Inc
Only 7 pages; contains no audit findings, control testing, or ISMS implementation details
No Statement of Applicability (SOA) included despite being referenced as Version 1.0

Certification body (Gradient Certification Inc) is accredited by UAF (United Accreditation Foundation), which is a lesser-known accreditation body — not IAF/ANAB/UKAS. This may indicate a less rigorous certification process
Zero technical or security details disclosed — no cloud, no architecture, no encryption, no access controls
Audit date (8/13/2025) is in the future relative to typical report availability, suggesting this is an in-process certification
yellow_flags: ISO 27001 reports typically contain less technical detail than SOC 2, but this document contains none at all because it is not an audit report
Cannot assess actual ISMS maturity without the completed audit report and SOA
Single-site certification (no additional sites), which is appropriate for a startup but limits assurance scope
Total audit man-days of 9 across the full 3-year cycle is on the low end, suggesting a small organization
green_flags: Company is pursuing ISO 27001 certification, indicating awareness of information security standards
ISMS scope is comprehensive in description: covers core product development, AI technology, data processing, IT infrastructure, HR, physical security, legal & compliance
Product addresses compliance/regulatory space, suggesting organizational familiarity with compliance frameworks
key_observations: This PDF is a certification agreement/contract, not an audit report. To perform meaningful tech due diligence, the actual ISO 27001 audit report (Stage-2 findings), the certificate itself, and the Statement of Applicability (SOA) would be needed.
Openwealth builds an AI-powered regulatory mapping tool — the product itself is in the compliance space, which may indicate above-average internal security awareness.
The certification body Gradient Certification Inc is based in Sheridan, WY (registered) and Houston, TX (corporate). UAF accreditation should be verified for legitimacy.
Areg Nzsdejan appears to be a key decision-maker (signed both the UAF endorsement and the certification agreement).
The 9 total man-days for the full certification cycle suggests a small company (likely under 25-50 employees based on ISO 27006 guidelines).
